key_events_next_24h.note: GDP missed expectations at 2.0% vs 2.2% consensus. Slight bearish pressure. PCE component key for inflation narrative. Less dire than Atlanta Fed 1.2% nowcast implied.

key_events_next_24h.note: First Friday of May. Strong jobs = less Fed cut pressure = bearish for BTC. Weak = Fed dovish pivot hope = bullish. Key binary catalyst within 24h.

analysis.btc_price_note: BTC ~$75,600-$76,300 on Apr 30. Down ~2% in 24h. Twice rejected at $80K resistance (double top). Support ~$74,900. Progressively lower opens each morning this week.
sentiment_key_signals: FOMC Apr 28-29: held 3.5-3.75%, hawkish 8-4 dissent — most divided since Oct 1992, tone opposed further cuts
Still within 48h post-FOMC window — historical BTC weakness pattern active
BTC twice rejected at $80K — firm technical ceiling (double top pattern)
Spot BTC ETF: -$137.77M on Apr 29 (second confirmed outflow day)
30-year US Treasury yield hit 5.0% — highest since Jul 2025; capital rotation away from risk
GDP Q1 2026: 2.0% advance estimate — missed 2.2% forecast, still recovery from Q4 0.5%
NFP May 1 at 12:30 UTC — next high-impact binary catalyst within 24h
Geopolitical: Iran / Strait of Hormuz tensions, oil elevated, risk-off sentiment
Bitcoin dominance high; altcoins fragile — market breadth narrow
analysis.macro_backdrop: Post-FOMC hawkish hold (3.5-3.75%, 8-4 split against further cuts language). 30yr yield at 5% signals sustained inflation concern and rate-cut delay. GDP Q1 missed at 2.0% vs 2.2%. ETF outflows second consecutive day. BTC double-topped at $80K. NFP May 1 is next major catalyst within 24h — bearish lean maintained until NFP confirms.
analysis.regime_rationale: Bearish (0.70): FOMC hawkish aftermath, $80K double rejection, consecutive ETF outflows, 30yr yield at 5%, GDP miss, Fear & Greed 33, declining weekly opens. Confidence 0.70 (>=0.5 threshold) — macro filter ACTIVE. Prefer SHORT bias or cautious HOLD until NFP May 1.
